CHARACTER THIAGO CORTEZ

SETTING MIAMI

SERIES LA CASA CORTEZ




Thiago Cortez carries power the way most men carry instinct, naturally, without effort, and without the need to prove it. As the head of La Casa Cortez, authority was never something he had to chase. He was raised in it, shaped by it, and now moves through it with the certainty of someone who has never known anything else. In Miami, a city built on excess, ambition, and quiet corruption, his presence blends seamlessly into the surface while controlling what lies beneath it. Where others rely on noise or spectacle, Thiago operates with intention. He watches first, acts second, and when he does, it is rarely without consequence. Power, to him, is not something to display constantly. It exists in control, in the ability to decide when something happens, and when it doesn’t. Most situations are settled long before they reach him, not because he avoids involvement, but because his name alone is often enough to prevent escalation. And when it isn’t, he has no hesitation in stepping in himself. His approach is direct, immediate, and final. There is a tension to Thiago that people notice quickly. He is not patient in the traditional sense. He does not wait out problems or soften his responses.
